# Atteler command surface

Schema: `atteler.cli.command-surface.v1`

## Chat & sessions

Run the TUI or one-shot prompts, manage saved sessions, transcripts, headless runs, artifacts, and multi-agent run records.

Commands:
- `run [prompt]`: start chat or run positional one-shot prompt text
- `once <prompt|--stdin>`: send one prompt and exit
- `list`: list saved sessions (dispatch: `list-sessions`)
- `tags`: list saved session tags (dispatch: `list-session-tags`)
- `search <query>`: search saved session metadata and transcripts (dispatch: `search-sessions`)
- `show <id-or-path>`: print one saved session as YAML (dispatch: `session-read`)
- `summary <id-or-path>`: print compact saved session metadata (dispatch: `session-read`)
- `replay <id-or-path>`: print a previous transcript (dispatch: `session-read`)
- `export <id-or-path>`: export a previous transcript (dispatch: `session-read`)
- `runs`: list review/speculation runs for --session (dispatch: `session-read`)
- `show-run <id|latest|review|speculation>`: show one review/speculation run as YAML (dispatch: `session-read`)
- `export-run <id|latest|review|speculation>`: export one review/speculation run (dispatch: `session-read`)
- `replay-run <id|latest|review|speculation>`: replay recorded run artifacts without provider calls (dispatch: `session-read`)
- `resume-run <id|latest|review|speculation>`: resume from recorded run artifacts without provider calls (dispatch: `session-read`)
- `messages`: list compact message records for --session (dispatch: `session-read`)
- `artifacts`: list artifact records for --session (dispatch: `session-read`)
- `failures`: list negative-knowledge records for --session (dispatch: `session-read`)
- `record-failure <approach>`: record negative knowledge on --session (dispatch: `session-write`)
- `record-artifact <path>`: record a useful artifact path on --session (dispatch: `session-write`)
- `merge-artifacts <path>`: merge selected-session artifacts into a provenance bundle (dispatch: `merge-artifacts`)
- `headless`: list headless runs; filter with --headless-status/--headless-max-age (dispatch: `headless-command`)
- `status-headless <id>`: show one headless run status (dispatch: `headless-command`)
- `cancel-headless <id>`: cancel one live headless run (dispatch: `headless-command`)
- `retry-headless <id>`: retry one terminal headless run (dispatch: `headless-command`)
- `recover-headless`: reconcile stale/orphaned/expired headless runs (dispatch: `headless-command`)
- `stream-headless <id>`: stream one headless run log (dispatch: `headless-command`)
- `cleanup-headless`: remove terminal headless runs older than --headless-max-age (dispatch: `headless-command`)

Examples:
- `atteler chat once "Explain this repository in one paragraph"`
- `atteler session list`
- `atteler session search "auth retry"`

## Configuration & diagnostics

Inspect config load order, templates, validation, lifecycle hooks, readiness, and version metadata.

Commands:
- `paths`: list config files in load order (dispatch: `list-config-paths`)
- `template`: print a starter YAML config (dispatch: `print-config-template`)
- `init <path>`: write a starter YAML config without overwriting (dispatch: `init-config`)
- `validate`: validate merged YAML/JSON config and importer warnings (dispatch: `validate-config`)
- `migrate`: migrate existing config/state files to the current schema (dispatch: `config-migrate`)
- `report`: print redacted config diagnostics for issue reports (dispatch: `config-report`)
- `explain [field-prefix]`: print merged config values with per-field provenance (dispatch: `explain-config`)
- `doctor`: run provider-aware readiness diagnostics (dispatch: `doctor`)
- `doctor-offline`: run offline readiness diagnostics; use --output json for CI (dispatch: `doctor-offline`)
- `state`: show interactive state path, revision, and selected preference sources (dispatch: `state-diagnostics`)
- `commands-json`: dump the inspectable CLI command surface as JSON (dispatch: `command-surface-json`)
- `commands-docs`: render command surface docs from the dispatch contract (dispatch: `command-surface-docs`)
- `hooks`: list supported lifecycle hook events (dispatch: `list-hook-events`)
- `hooks-json`: list hook events as JSON (dispatch: `list-hook-events`)
- `version`: print build version information (dispatch: `show-version`)

Examples:
- `atteler config paths`
- `atteler config validate`
- `atteler config migrate`
- `atteler config report`

## Providers & models

Choose models, inspect provider inventories, tune generation settings, and preview routing decisions.

Commands:
- `list`: list built-in provider names without API calls (dispatch: `list-providers`)
- `known-models`: list built-in provider/model IDs without API calls (dispatch: `list-known-models`)
- `models`: list models discovered from configured providers (dispatch: `list-models`)
- `resolve <model>`: explain how a model ID resolves to a provider (dispatch: `explain-model-resolution`)
- `ollama-status`: print Ollama daemon lifecycle status without starting it (dispatch: `ollama-status`)
- `ollama-stop`: stop and clean up an Atteler-owned Ollama daemon (dispatch: `ollama-stop`)
- `route-interactive`: rank --route-candidate values for low TTFT (dispatch: `route-models-providerless`)
- `route-batch`: rank --route-candidate values for batch/cost preference (dispatch: `route-models-providerless`)

Examples:
- `atteler providers list`
- `atteler providers known-models`
- `atteler providers models`
- `atteler providers resolve gpt-5.5`
- `atteler providers ollama-status`
- `atteler providers ollama-stop`

## Agents & orchestration

Inspect agents, plan matching agents, manage task lists, spawn sub-agents, and preview speculative/async workflows.

Commands:
- `list`: list configured agents (dispatch: `list-agents`)
- `performance`: summarize saved-session agent performance (dispatch: `agent-performance-summary`)
- `describe <name>`: print one configured agent as YAML (dispatch: `describe-agent`)
- `plan <prompt>`: preview configured agents for a prompt (dispatch: `plan-agents-providerless`)
- `prompt-complete <input>`: preview local prompt completions with context freshness (dispatch: `prompt-complete-providerless`)
- `task-list`: list persistent agent tasks (dispatch: `task-command`)
- `task-add <title>`: add a persistent agent task (dispatch: `task-command`)
- `task-assign <id:agent>`: assign a persistent task (dispatch: `task-command`)
- `task-complete <id>`: mark a persistent task complete (dispatch: `task-command`)
- `async-plan`: print dependency-aware async task batches (dispatch: `async-plan`)
- `async-run`: execute dependency-aware async tasks (dispatch: `async-run`)
- `spawn <agent|prompt>`: spawn sub-agent one-shot prompts (dispatch: `spawn-agents`)
- `speculate-plan`: print a speculative three-round plan (dispatch: `speculate-plan`)
- `speculate-run`: execute the speculative three-round pipeline (dispatch: `speculate-run`)
- `feedback`: derive improvement proposals from --session (dispatch: `feedback-proposals`)
- `feedback-apply <config>`: record selected-session feedback proposals as pending guidance (dispatch: `session-write`)
- `feedback-approve <config>`: approve pending feedback guidance with --feedback-approve-agent and --feedback-approve-id (dispatch: `feedback-approve`)
- `feedback-rollback <config>`: roll back feedback guidance with --feedback-rollback-agent and --feedback-rollback-id (dispatch: `feedback-rollback`)
- `skill-suggest <step>`: suggest reviewed skill directories from repeated workflows (dispatch: `suggest-skill`)
- `skill-learning-list`: list automatically generated skills (dispatch: `suggest-skill`)
- `skill-learning-show <slug>`: print one automatically generated skill (dispatch: `suggest-skill`)
- `skill-learning-edit <slug>`: open one generated skill in $VISUAL or $EDITOR (dispatch: `suggest-skill`)
- `skill-learning-disable <slug>`: disable updates for one generated skill (dispatch: `suggest-skill`)
- `skill-learning-enable <slug>`: enable updates for one generated skill (dispatch: `suggest-skill`)
- `skill-learning-delete <slug>`: delete one generated skill (dispatch: `suggest-skill`)
- `skill-learning-disable-all`: opt out of automatic skill learning (dispatch: `suggest-skill`)
- `skill-learning-enable-all`: enable automatic skill learning (dispatch: `suggest-skill`)
- `bash <command>`: run an explicit local bash command (dispatch: `bash-command`)

Examples:
- `atteler agents list`
- `atteler agents plan "review auth changes"`
- `atteler agents task-list`

## Research

Create local-first cited research run artifacts for technical decisions, architecture exploration, dependency evaluation, and planning.

Commands:
- `run <question>`: gather project guidance and supplied sources into a cited research report (dispatch: `research-run`)

Examples:
- `atteler research run "Compare approaches for plugin sandboxing in Go CLIs"`
- `atteler research run --trusted-source go.dev --trusted-source github.com --deny-source example-content-farm.com --warn-low-trust "Research best practices for safe agent worktrees"`
- `atteler research run --output .atteler/research/plugin-sandboxing --generate-tasks "Find viable implementation approaches for sandboxing Atteler plugins"`

## Autoresearch

Run a headless worktree loop that proposes code experiments, validates them, and keeps only improvements.

Commands:
- `run <mission>`: start an autonomous code experiment loop for a hard or long task

Examples:
- `atteler autoresearch run "Improve agent-loop recovery; keep only changes that pass make test"`
- `atteler autoresearch "Reduce prompt-context cache misses and validate with go test ./cmd/atteler"`
- `atteler session headless`

## Issue implementation

Run the autonomous Symphony issue-to-PR agent with explicit verification gates.

Commands:
- `implement <issue-ref>`: implement one tracker issue and optionally open a verified pull request (dispatch: `issue-implement`)

Examples:
- `atteler issue implement GH-218 --open-pr`
- `atteler issue implement https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/218 --open-pr`
- `atteler issue implement GH-218 --open-pr --base main --run-tests --run-lint`

## Memory & Retrieval

Search saved sessions, UTF-8 file memory stores, workspace vector indexes, agent vector memory, local lexical/embedding indexes, and git history.

Commands:
- `search <query>`: search scoped local memory and report the searched corpus (dispatch: `memory-command`)
- `retrieve <query>`: search selected/filtered sources under the unified retrieval contract (dispatch: `retrieval-search`)
- `index <file>`: add a file to the local memory store (dispatch: `memory-command`, `retrieval-search`)
- `purge <selector>`: purge memory docs by session:<id>, tag:<tag>, repo:<path>, or all (dispatch: `memory-command`)
- `rebuild`: rebuild the JSON memory store from the selected corpus (dispatch: `memory-command`)
- `list-corpus`: print memory corpus metadata (dispatch: `memory-command`)
- `agent-search <query>`: search one agent's vector memory (dispatch: `agent-memory`)
- `agent-index <file>`: add a file to one agent's vector memory (dispatch: `agent-memory`)
- `agent-delete <id>`: delete one document from one agent's vector memory (dispatch: `agent-memory`)
- `agent-compact`: remove expired documents from per-agent vector memory (dispatch: `agent-memory`)
- `agent-migrate`: explicitly migrate and re-vectorize per-agent memory (dispatch: `agent-memory`)
- `vector-search <query>`: search a persisted lexical-fallback or embedding vector index (dispatch: `vector-search`)
- `vector-index <file>`: chunk and add a file to the persisted vector index (dispatch: `vector-search`, `retrieval-search`)
- `git-history <query>`: search local git history subjects/files/authors (dispatch: `git-history-search-providerless`)
- `context-pack <path>`: compact a role-prefixed transcript file (dispatch: `context-pack`)

Examples:
- `atteler memory search "OAuth retry storm"`
- `atteler memory retrieve "OAuth retry storm"`
- `atteler memory retrieve "OAuth retry storm" --retrieval-source vector`
- `atteler memory git-history "memory regression"`
- `atteler memory vector-search "redirect risks"`
- `atteler memory vector-index docs/research.md`

## Code intelligence

Run Go code index, import graph, package/file/symbol, impact, and optional LSP queries without an LLM call; add --json or --output json for the stable schema.

Commands:
- `summary`: print compact Go code index counts (dispatch: `code-intel`)
  - Text output: `files=<n>\tpackages=<n>\tsymbols=<n>\timports=<n>\tnodes=<n>\tedges=<n>\tcycles=<n>\tlayers=<n>`
  - JSON schema: `atteler.code_intel.v1`
  - JSON fields: `schema`, `command`, `query`, `empty`, `message`, `summary`
  - Examples: `atteler code-intel summary`
- `query <kind[:value]>`: query the shared multi-language code-intelligence index (dispatch: `code-intel`)
  - Text output: `type=<record>\tlanguage=<language>[\tname=<name>][\tkind=<kind>][\tpath=<path>][\tline=<n>]...`
  - JSON schema: `atteler.code_intel.v1`
  - JSON fields: `schema`, `command`, `query`, `empty`, `message`, `pagination`, `records`
  - Examples: `atteler code-intel query definitions:Run`
- `files`: list Go files with package/import/symbol counts (dispatch: `code-intel`)
  - Text output: `path=<path>\tpackage=<package>\tsymbols=<n>\timports=<n>`
  - JSON schema: `atteler.code_intel.v1`
  - JSON fields: `schema`, `command`, `query`, `empty`, `message`, `pagination`, `files`
  - Examples: `atteler code-intel files`
- `packages`: list Go packages with file and symbol counts (dispatch: `code-intel`)
  - Text output: `package=<package>\tfiles=<n>\tsymbols=<n>`
  - JSON schema: `atteler.code_intel.v1`
  - JSON fields: `schema`, `command`, `query`, `empty`, `message`, `pagination`, `packages`

- `package <package>`: list files and symbol counts for one package (dispatch: `code-intel`)

  - JSON schema: `atteler.code_intel.v1`

- `package-imports <package>`: list import usage counts for one package (dispatch: `code-intel`)

## Incidents

Diagnose production incidents from observability sources and prepare test-first fixes with redacted reports.

Commands:
- `diagnose`: fetch incident context, link stack traces to code, plan reproduction/tests/fix, and optionally prepare a PR body (dispatch: `incident-diagnose-providerless`, `incident-diagnose`)

Examples:
- `atteler incident diagnose --sentry ISSUE-912`
- `atteler incident diagnose --incident-file redacted-sentry-event.json`
- `atteler incident diagnose --sentry ISSUE-912 --incident-apply-fix --incident-validation-command "go test ./pkg/auth"`

## Review

Scan the current repository or plan/run review-agent workflows with explicit gates.

Commands:
- `scan`: scan the current repository and print a structured review report (dispatch: `review-scan-providerless`)
- `plan`: print speculative review-agent plan (dispatch: `review-plan`)
- `run`: execute the review-agent three-round pipeline (dispatch: `review-run`)

Examples:
- `atteler review scan`
- `atteler review plan`
- `atteler review run`

## Watch

Scan or continuously watch repository health findings for background-agent workflows.

Commands:
- `scan`: scan once for background-agent health findings (dispatch: `watch-scan-providerless`)
- `json`: scan once and emit findings as JSON (dispatch: `watch-scan-providerless`)
- `loop`: continuously scan until interrupted or max iterations (dispatch: `watch-loop-providerless`)

Examples:
- `atteler watch scan`
- `atteler watch json`
- `atteler watch loop`

## Plugins & MCP

Inspect, run, scaffold plugins, and validate or invoke MCP manifests.

Commands:
- `list`: list configured local plugin manifests (dispatch: `list-plugins`)
- `describe <name>`: print one configured plugin manifest (dispatch: `describe-plugin`)
- `run <plugin[/entrypoint]>`: execute a configured plugin entrypoint (dispatch: `run-plugin-providerless`)
- `init-rtk <dir>`: write an RTK plugin scaffold (dispatch: `init-rtk-plugin`)
- `mcp-manifest <path>`: validate/list an MCP manifest (dispatch: `mcp-manifest`)
- `mcp-tool <tool>`: invoke an MCP tool through tools/call (dispatch: `mcp-invoke`)
- `mcp-method <method>`: invoke a JSON-RPC method on --mcp-server (dispatch: `mcp-invoke`)

Examples:
- `atteler plugins list`
- `atteler plugins run reviewer/check`
- `atteler plugins manifest .atteler/mcp.yaml`

## Worktrees

Run sessions in isolated git worktrees; worktrees are preserved by default and merge-back is review-gated.

Commands:
- `run [prompt]`: enable worktree isolation for this session; add --worktree-auto-merge plus --worktree-verify-command to merge on exit
- `list`: list active atteler worktrees (dispatch: `list-worktrees`)
- `merge <session-id>`: manually merge a session worktree back into its base branch; add --worktree-verify-command to run checks first or --merge-worktree-allow-base-mismatch to override the recorded-base preflight (dispatch: `merge-worktree`)

Examples:
- `atteler worktrees run "Add unit tests for auth"`
- `atteler worktrees list`
- `atteler worktrees merge 20260430-120000-deadbeef`

## Evaluation & fixtures

Compare deterministic outputs with text or structured assertions, record/replay one-shot response fixtures, and record agent evaluations.

Commands:
- `output <path>`: compare actual output against expected text/file (dispatch: `eval-output`)
- `run <path>`: run a structured YAML/JSON assertion file (dispatch: `eval-output`)
- `fixtures <dir>`: discover and run structured eval fixtures in a directory (dispatch: `eval-output`)
- `list`: list agent evaluations on --session (dispatch: `session-read`)
- `record <agent>`: append an evaluation to --session (dispatch: `session-write`)
- `record-response <path> <prompt|--stdin>`: run one prompt and write request/response JSON
- `replay-response <path> <prompt|--stdin>`: run one prompt from a recorded response JSON

Examples:
- `atteler eval output .atteler/fixtures/readme-summary.txt --eval-expected "package overview"`
- `atteler eval run .atteler/evals/readme.eval.yaml`
- `atteler eval fixtures .atteler/evals --eval-report .atteler/eval-report.json`
- `atteler eval record reviewer`
- `atteler eval replay-response .atteler/fixtures/once.json "Summarize @README.md"`

## Interactive slash commands

Slash help:

```text
/help /model [name] /profile [name] /save /export [path] /clear /retry /edit /fork [n]
/tokens /cost /search <query> /pin <n> /unpin <n> /context [prune] /mode [plan|execute] /suggestions [status|local|session|folder|global]
/template [name] /codeblocks /save-code <n> <path> /copy [last|session] /copy-code [n] /apply-patch /eval add|run
```

Commands:
- `/help`: show interactive slash command help
  - Input: `slashNoArgsInput`
  - Completion tokens: `commands`
  - Side effects: `stdout`
  - Outputs: `text`
- `/model [name]`: show or change the active model
  - Input: `slashOptionalValueInput`
  - Input fields: `Value`
  - Arguments: `name:string optional`
  - Completion tokens: `provider`
  - Side effects: `session-store-write`, `stdout`
  - Outputs: `text`
  - Policy: `mutates-session-store`
- `/profile [name]`: show or change the configured agent profile
  - Input: `slashOptionalValueInput`
  - Input fields: `Value`
  - Arguments: `name:string optional`
  - Completion tokens: `agent`
  - Side effects: `config-read`, `session-store-write`, `stdout`
  - Outputs: `text`
  - Policy: `mutates-session-store`
- `/save`: save the current session
  - Input: `slashNoArgsInput`
  - Side effects: `session-store-write`, `stdout`
  - Outputs: `text`
  - Policy: `mutates-session-store`
- `/export [path]`: export the current session transcript
  - Input: `slashOptionalValueInput`
  - Input fields: `Value`
  - Arguments: `path:string optional`
  - Shared CLI commands: `session-read`
  - Completion tokens: `session`
  - Side effects: `session-store-read`, `stdout`, `filesystem-write`
  - Outputs: `text`, `yaml`, `markdown`, `json`, `filesystem`
  - Policy: `mutates-filesystem`
- `/clear`: clear the visible conversation and persist the empty session
  - Input: `slashNoArgsInput`
  - Side effects: `session-store-write`, `stdout`
  - Outputs: `text`
  - Policy: `mutates-conversation`, `mutates-session-store`
- `/retry`: regenerate the last user prompt
  - Input: `slashNoArgsInput`
  - Aliases: `regenerate`
  - Completion tokens: `regenerate`
  - Side effects: `llm-provider-read`, `session-store-write`, `stdout`
  - Outputs: `text`
  - Policy: `mutates-conversation`, `mutates-session-store`, `calls-llm`
- `/edit`: edit the last user prompt
  - Input: `slashNoArgsInput`
  - Side effects: `session-store-write`, `stdout`
  - Outputs: `text`
  - Policy: `mutates-conversation`
- `/fork [n]`: fork the current session at a message index
  - Input: `slashForkInput`
  - Input fields: `Count`, `HasCount`
  - Arguments: `n:int optional`
  - Completion tokens: `session`
  - Side effects: `session-store-write`, `stdout`
  - Outputs: `text`
  - Policy: `mutates-conversation`
- `/tokens`: show token usage and estimated cost
  - Input: `slashNoArgsInput`
  - Aliases: `cost`
  - Help aliases: `cost`
  - Completion tokens: `cost`
  - Side effects: `stdout`
  - Outputs: `text`
- `/search <query>`: search the current conversation
  - Input: `slashSearchInput`
  - Input fields: `Query`
  - Arguments: `query:string required, variadic`
  - Completion tokens: `session`
  - Side effects: `stdout`
  - Outputs: `text`
- `/pin <n>`: pin a message before pruning context
  - Input: `slashMessageNumberInput`
  - Input fields: `Number`
  - Arguments: `n:int required`
  - Completion tokens: `context`
  - Side effects: `stdout`
  - Outputs: `text`
  - Policy: `mutates-conversation`
- `/unpin <n>`: unpin a message
  - Input: `slashMessageNumberInput`
  - Input fields: `Number`
  - Arguments: `n:int required`
  - Completion tokens: `context`
  - Side effects: `stdout`
  - Outputs: `text`
  - Policy: `mutates-conversation`
- `/context [prune]`: show or prune conversation context
  - Input: `slashContextInput`
  - Input fields: `Prune`
  - Arguments: `prune:enum(prune) optional`
  - Side effects: `stdout`
  - Outputs: `text`
  - Policy: `mutates-conversation`
- `/mode [plan|execute]`: show or switch between plan and execute modes
  - Input: `slashModeInput`
  - Input fields: `Mode`, `Show`
  - Arguments: `mode:enum(plan|execute) optional`
  - Completion tokens: `plan`, `execute`
  - Side effects: `stdout`
  - Outputs: `text`
  - Policy: `mutates-conversation`
- `/suggestions [status|local|session|folder|global]`: show or opt in to model-backed idle prompt suggestions
  - Input: `slashSuggestionsInput`
  - Input fields: `Mode`, `Show`
  - Arguments: `mode:enum(status|local|session|folder|global) optional`
  - Completion tokens: `prompt`, `privacy`, `local`, `provider`
  - Side effects: `llm-provider-read`, `session-store-write`, `state-store-write`, `stdout`
  - Outputs: `text`
  - Policy: `mutates-session-store`, `calls-llm`
- `/template [name]`: insert a local prompt template
  - Input: `slashOptionalValueInput`
  - Input fields: `Value`
  - Arguments: `name:string optional`
  - Completion tokens: `prompt`
  - Side effects: `stdout`
  - Outputs: `text`
- `/codeblocks`: list code blocks from the last assistant response
  - Input: `slashNoArgsInput`
  - Completion tokens: `code`
  - Side effects: `stdout`
  - Outputs: `text`
- `/save-code <n> <path>`: save a code block from the last assistant response
  - Input: `slashSaveCodeInput`
  - Input fields: `Path`, `Block`
  - Arguments: `n:int required`, `path:string required`
  - Completion tokens: `code`
  - Side effects: `filesystem-write`, `stdout`
  - Outputs: `filesystem`, `text`
  - Policy: `mutates-filesystem`
- `/copy [last|session]`: copy the last answer or full session
  - Input: `slashCopyInput`
  - Input fields: `Target`
  - Arguments: `target:enum(last|session) optional`
  - Side effects: `process-execute`, `stdout`
  - Outputs: `process`, `text`
  - Policy: `uses-clipboard`
- `/copy-code [n]`: copy a code block, defaulting to the first block
  - Input: `slashCopyCodeInput`
  - Input fields: `Block`
  - Arguments: `n:int optional`
  - Completion tokens: `code`
  - Side effects: `process-execute`, `stdout`
  - Outputs: `process`, `text`
  - Policy: `uses-clipboard`
- `/apply-patch`: apply the last assistant unified diff with git apply
  - Input: `slashNoArgsInput`
  - Completion tokens: `patch`
  - Side effects: `process-execute`, `worktree-write`, `stdout`
  - Outputs: `process`, `text`
  - Policy: `runs-local-process`, `mutates-worktree`
- `/eval add|run`: add or count local evaluation cases
  - Input: `slashEvalInput`
  - Input fields: `Action`
  - Arguments: `action:enum(add|run) required`
  - Completion tokens: `test`
  - Variants:
    - `/eval add`: add the current session to local evaluation cases
      - Side effects: `filesystem-write`, `session-store-read`, `stdout`
      - Outputs: `filesystem`, `text`
      - Policy: `mutates-filesystem`
    - `/eval run`: count local evaluation cases
      - Side effects: `filesystem-read`, `stdout`
      - Outputs: `text`
  - Side effects: `filesystem-write`, `session-store-read`, `stdout`, `filesystem-read`
  - Outputs: `filesystem`, `text`
  - Policy: `mutates-filesystem`

## Dispatch commands

- `list-hook-events` (providerless): list supported lifecycle hook event types
  - Input: `listHookEventsCommandInput`
  - Input fields: `JSON`
  - Flags: `--list-hook-events`, `--list-hook-events-json`
  - Examples: `atteler config hooks-json`
  - Conflicts:
    - `exclusive-command` with `*`: command-triggering flags are mutually exclusive unless an explicit precedence rule declares otherwise
  - Side effects: `stdout`
  - Outputs: `text`, `json`
  - Fixtures:
    - `legacy-flag`: `atteler --list-hook-events` -> `list-hook-events`
- `headless-command` (providerless): inspect, reconcile, stream, cancel, retry, or clean up headless runs
  - Input: `headlessCommandInput`
  - Input fields: `StatusID`, `CancelID`, `RetryID`, `RetryNewID`, `StreamID`, `StatusFilter`, `MaxAge`, `Recover`, `List`, `Cleanup`
  - Flags: `--list-headless`, `--recover-headless`, `--status-headless`, `--cancel-headless`, `--retry-headless`, `--retry-headless-id`, `--stream-headless`, `--cleanup-headless`, `--headless-status`, `--headless-max-age`
  - Examples: `atteler session headless`, `atteler session status-headless run-123`, `atteler session cancel-headless run-123`, `atteler session retry-headless run-123`, `atteler session recover-headless`, `atteler session stream-headless run-123`, `atteler session cleanup-headless --headless-max-age 168h`
  - Conflicts:
    - `exclusive-command` with `*`: command-triggering flags are mutually exclusive unless an explicit precedence rule declares otherwise
  - Side effects: `session-store-read`, `session-store-write`, `stdout`, `process-execute`
  - Outputs: `text`
  - Fixtures:
    - `legacy-flag`: `atteler --list-headless` -> `headless-command`
- `list-sessions` (providerless): list saved sessions
  - Input: `listSessionsCommandInput`
  - Input fields: `Tag`
  - Flags: `--list-sessions`, `--list-sessions-tag`
  - Examples: `atteler session list`
  - Conflicts:
    - `exclusive-command` with `*`: command-triggering flags are mutually exclusive unless an explicit precedence rule declares otherwise
  - Side effects: `session-store-read`, `stdout`
  - Outputs: `text`
  - Fixtures:
    - `legacy-flag`: `atteler --list-sessions` -> `list-sessions`
- `list-session-tags` (providerless): list saved session tags with counts
  - Input: `listSessionTagsCommandInput`
  - Flags: `--list-session-tags`
  - Examples: `atteler session tags`
  - Conflicts:
    - `exclusive-command` with `*`: command-triggering flags are mutually exclusive unless an explicit precedence rule declares otherwise
  - Side effects: `session-store-read`, `stdout`
  - Outputs: `text`
  - Fixtures:
    - `legacy-flag`: `atteler --list-session-tags` -> `list-session-tags`
- `agent-performance-summary` (providerless): summarize recorded agent performance across saved sessions
  - Input: `agentPerformanceSummaryCommandInput`
  - Flags: `--agent-performance-summary`
  - Examples: `atteler agents performance`
  - Conflicts:
    - `exclusive-command` with `*`: command-triggering flags are mutually exclusive unless an explicit precedence rule declares otherwise
  - Side effects: `session-store-read`, `stdout`
  - Outputs: `text`
  - Fixtures:
    - `legacy-flag`: `atteler --agent-performance-summary` -> `agent-performance-summary`
- `search-sessions` (providerless): search saved session metadata and transcripts
  - Input: `searchSessionsCommandInput`
  - Input fields: `Query`
  - Flags: `--search-sessions`
  - Examples: `atteler session search "auth retry"`
  - Conflicts:
    - `exclusive-command` with `*`: command-triggering flags are mutually exclusive unless an explicit precedence rule declares otherwise
  - Side effects: `session-store-read`, `stdout`
  - Outputs: `text`
  - Fixtures:
    - `legacy-flag`: `atteler --search-sessions value` -> `search-sessions`
- `research-run` (providerless): create local-first cited research run artifacts
  - Input: `researchCommandInput`
  - Input fields: `Question`, `OutputDir`, `TrustedSources`, `DeniedSources`, `Sources`, `WarnLowTrust`, `GenerateTasks`
  - Flags: `--research-run`, `--research-output`, `--trusted-source`, `--deny-source`, `--research-source`, `--warn-low-trust`, `--output`, `--generate-tasks`
  - Examples: `atteler research run "Compare approaches for plugin sandboxing in Go CLIs"`, `atteler research run --output .atteler/research/plugin-sandboxing --generate-tasks "Find viable implementation approaches for sandboxing Atteler plugins"`
  - Conflicts:
    - `exclusive-command` with `*`: command-triggering flags are mutually exclusive unless an explicit precedence rule declares otherwise
  - Side effects: `filesystem-read`, `filesystem-write`, `stdout`
  - Outputs: `markdown`, `json`, `yaml`, `filesystem`, `text`
  - Fixtures:
    - `legacy-flag`: `atteler --research-run value` -> `research-run`
- `task-command` (providerless): read or mutate persistent agent tasks

- `incident-diagnose-providerless` (providerless-config): diagnose a production incident from Sentry, MCP, or redacted JSON without starting the repair agent loop
  - Input: `incidentDiagnoseOnlyCommandInput`
  - Input fields: `SentryIssue`, `Reference`, `FilePath`, `SentryOrg`, `SentryBaseURL`, `SentryTokenEnv`, `SentryEventID`, `MCPManifestPath`, `MCPServerName`, `MCPToolName`, `MCPToolArgsJSON`, `ReproCommand`, `ValidationCommands`, `ReportPath`, `PRBodyPath`, `OutputFormat`, `TimeoutSeconds`, `JSON`
  - Flags: `--incident-diagnose`, `--sentry`, `--incident-ref`, `--incident-file`, `--incident-sentry-org`, `--incident-sentry-url`, `--incident-sentry-token-env`, `--incident-sentry-event`, `--incident-mcp-manifest`, `--incident-mcp-server`, `--incident-mcp-tool`, `--incident-mcp-tool-args`, `--incident-repro-command`, `--incident-validation-command`, `--incident-timeout-seconds`, `--incident-report`, `--incident-pr-body`, `--output`, `--json`
  - Examples: `atteler incident diagnose --sentry ISSUE-912`, `atteler incident diagnose --incident-file redacted-sentry-event.json --incident-report .atteler/incident.md`, `atteler incident diagnose --incident-ref alert-42 --incident-mcp-manifest .atteler/mcp.yaml --incident-mcp-server grafana --incident-mcp-tool get_incident`
